{"query":"Pope Leo XIV Liberty Medal July 4 2026 awarding institution ceremony location","model":"mako","response_time_ms":1581,"verdict":null,"extra_info":{"type":"featured_snippet","text":"(June 23, 2026) – On July 3, 2026, the eve of the nation's 250th anniversary, the National Constitution Center (NCC) will present Pope Leo XIV with the 38th annual Liberty Medal during a ceremony at Philadelphia's Independence Mall."},"results":[{"url":"https://www.villanova.edu/university/media/press-releases/2026/liberty-medal.html#:~:text=(June%2023%2C%202026)%20%E2%80%93,ceremony%20at%20Philadelphia's%20Independence%20Mall.","title":"Pope Leo XIV to Receive 38th Annual Liberty Medal from the National Constitution Center | Villanova University","content":"The honor reflects the values that have shaped Pope Leo’s life and leadership: his Augustinian formation, global pastoral experience and American roots.
Villanova University, an Augustinian Catholic institution and one of the event's co-sponsors, shares a special connection with His Holiness.
The location of the Liberty Medal ceremony, as well as this year's honoree, underscores the historic ties between the ideals of the nation’s founding and the Augustinian tradition.
Augustine's Church, the first Augustinian church in the United States.
Augustine's Church and the temporary closure of the College, illustrating the challenges religious communities faced even as the young nation worked to realize its promise of religious freedom."},{"url":"https://constitutioncenter.org/about/liberty-medal","title":"Liberty Medal | Constitution Center","content":"As the nation marked its 250th anniversary, the ceremony brought together civic and faith leaders, as well as visitors from Philadelphia, across the nation, and around the world, to reflect on how the promise that individuals may worship freely, speak openly, and live according to their own convictions has strengthened civic life in the United States and inspired movements for human dignity and freedom around the globe.
His Holiness Pope Leo XIV, born Robert Francis Prevost on September 14, 1955, in Chicago, Ill., is the first pope from the Order of Saint Augustine and the first U.S.-born pontiff.
He completed theological studies at the Catholic Theological Union in Chicago and was ordained a priest on June 19, 1982, in Rome.
Pope Francis appointed Father Prevost apostolic administrator of the Diocese of Chiclayo, Peru, in 2014, and he was ordained a bishop later that year.
In 2023, Pope Francis called him to Rome to serve as prefect of the Dicastery for Bishops and president of the Pontifical Commission for Latin America, placing him at the center of the Church’s global leadership and episcopal appointments."},{"url":"https://july4thphilly.com/event/liberty-medal/","title":"38th Annual Liberty Medal Ceremony - Wawa Welcome America","content":"The National Constitution Center will award the 38th annual Liberty Medal to His Holiness Pope Leo XIV at a public ceremony held inside the National Constitution Center!
Pope Leo XIV intends to deliver live acceptance remarks virtually from the Vatican, which will be live streamed to those in attendance on Independence Mall, as well as online via the Center’s website.
This special Liberty Medal ceremony, held in celebration of the nation’s 250th anniversary and semiquincentennial, is presented in partnership with the City of Philadelphia and Wawa Welcome America as part of the city’s Independence Week events celebrating the semiquincentennial of the signing of the Declaration of Independence.
The ceremony will be streamed live to audiences gathered on Independence Mall, with Comcast NBCUniversal supporting the livestream on site."},{"url":"https://constitutioncenter.org/about/liberty-medal/tickets-sponsorships","title":"Tickets & Sponsorships | Constitution Center","content":"Join us on July 3, 2026, the eve of the 250th anniversary of the founding of the United States, as we honor His Holiness Pope Leo XIV."}]}

{"query":"Pope Leo XIV Liberty Medal July 4 2026 awarding institution ceremony location","follow_up_questions":null,"answer":null,"images":[],"results":[{"url":"https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=-8Q1QLpV99A","title":"2026 Liberty Medal Ceremony Honoring Pope Leo XIV","content":"2026 Liberty Medal Ceremony Honoring Pope Leo XIV
National Constitution Center
68200 subscribers
976 likes
31189 views
3 Jul 2026
0:00 Introduction
2:25 Welcome - Vince Stango, Interim President & CEO, National Constitution Center
4:00 Remarks - The Honorable Cherelle L. Parker, Mayor, City of Philadelphia
8:32 Ringing of the National Liberty Museum's Replica Liberty Bell, Led by National Liberty Museum President and CEO Dr. Alaine K. Arnott and Pennsylvania Attorney General Dave Sunday
9:50 Remarks - Pennsylvania Attorney General Dave Sunday
12:33 Remarks - Vince Stango, Interim President & CEO, National Constitution Center
17:52 Remarks - The Reverend Carolyn C. Cavaness, Pastor, Mother Bethel AME Church;
Board Member, Interfaith Philadelphia
20:23 Remarks - Imam Quaiser D. Abdullah, Ph.D, Director, Mayor’s Office of Muslim Engagement; Board Member, Interfaith Philadelphia 
25:00 Remarks - Rabbi Jill L. Maderer, Senior Rabbi, Congregation Rodeph Shalom;
Board Member, Interfaith Philadelphia
28:56 Remarks - The Reverend Luis A. Cortes Jr., Founder, President, CEO of Esperanza,
Hispanic Clergy of Philadelphia, Member of Religious Leaders Council of Greater Philadelphia
32:37 Remarks - The Most Reverend Nelson J. Perez, D.D., Archbishop of Philadelphia; Co-Convener, Religious Leaders Council, Interfaith Philadelphia 
37:11 Remarks - The Honorable Josh Shapiro, Governor, Commonwealth of Pennsylvania
44:14 Presentation of the Liberty Medal - Video of Delegation at the Vatican 
49:05 Acceptance Remarks - His Holiness Pope Leo XIV, Live Address from the Vatican
59:52 Closing - Vince Stango, Interim President & CEO, National Constitution Center
1:01:40 "America, the Beautiful" - Performed by Cathedral Basilica Choir, Archdiocesan Choir of Philadelphia


On July 3, 2026, the eve of the 250th anniversary of the founding of the United States, the National Constitution Center awarded the 38th annual Liberty Medal to His Holiness Pope Leo XIV for his lifelong work promoting religious liberty and freedom of conscience and expression around the world—ideals enshrined by America's founders in the First Amendment to the U.S. Constitution. The ceremony took place at the National Constitution Center, steps from Independence Hall, where the Declaration of Independence and the U.S. Constitution were signed. As the nation marks its 250th anniversary, the ceremony brought together civic and faith leaders, as well as visitors from Philadelphia, across the nation, and around the world, to reflect on how the promise that individuals may worship freely, speak openly, and live according to their own convictions has strengthened civic life in the United States and inspired movements for human dignity and freedom around the globe. Pope Leo XIV delivered live acceptance remarks virtually from the Vatican.

Below is a list of confirmed ceremony participants:

His Holiness Pope Leo XIV (virtual)
Vince Stango, Interim President and CEO, National Constitution Center
Imam Quaiser D. Abdullah, Ph.D., Director, Mayor’s Office of Muslim Engagement; Board Member, Interfaith Philadelphia
The Reverend Carolyn C. Cavaness, Pastor, Mother Bethel AME Church; Board Member, Interfaith Philadelphia
The Reverend Luis A. Cortés Jr., Founder, President, CEO of Esperanza, Hispanic Clergy of Philadelphia, Member of Religious Leaders Council of Greater Philadelphia 
Rabbi Jill L. Maderer, Senior Rabbi, Congregation Rodeph Shalom; Board Member, Interfaith Philadelphia
The Most Reverend Nelson J. Pérez, D.D., Archbishop of Philadelphia; Co-Convener, Religious Leaders Council, Interfaith Philadelphia
The Cathedral Basilica Choir and The Archdiocesan Choir of Philadelphia
Additional civic and community leaders to be announced

The Liberty Medal is made possible thanks to support from corporations, foundations, and individuals with proceeds going to further the work of the National Constitution Center. Thank you to prize sponsors Ira M. Lubert & Pamela Estadt, and to the 2026 Liberty Medal underwriters:

Citizens 
Doug and Maria DeVos
The James and Agnes Kim Foundation
Kenneth C. Griffin
Griffin Catalyst

The 2026 Liberty Medal Ceremony is made possible by:

City of Philadelphia 
Comcast NBCUniversal
Wawa Welcome America 
Interfaith Philadelphia
Villanova University

For a full list of sponsors, please visit https://constitutioncenter.org/about/liberty-medal 

Explore additional America’s 250th resources https://constitutioncenter.org/america-at-250 

Subscribe to the National Constitution Center on YouTube: https://www.youtube.com/user/ConstitutionCenter?sub_confirmation=1

Register for upcoming programs: https://constitutioncenter.org/news-debate/americas-town-hall-programs

Visit our media library to discover more online classes, podcasts, and Town Hall conversations: https://constitutioncenter.org/news-debate/media-library 

Follow the National Constitution Center on social media:
 
Facebook: https://www.facebook.com/constitutionctr/ 

X: https://x.com/ConstitutionCtr
